title: JPMorgan Chase Financial Summary From 2025 Annual Report
text: JPMorgan Chase's 2025 financial highlights show the scale and profitability of the firm. Total net revenue was $182.4 billion, compared with $177.6 billion in 2024, and net income was $57.0 billion. Diluted earnings per share were $20.02. Net interest income was $95.4 billion, supported by the firm's large deposit, loan, card, and balance sheet businesses. At year-end 2025, total assets were $4.4 trillion, loans were $1.49 trillion, deposits were $2.56 trillion, and total stockholders' equity was $362.4 billion. The firm reported a standardized common equity Tier 1 capital ratio of 14.6% and a return on tangible common equity of 20%. Main risks include credit losses, deposit competition, interest-rate changes, capital requirements, market volatility, cyber and operational risk, geopolitical uncertainty, regulatory changes, and the cyclicality of investment banking and markets revenue.
